dataset: LIDC-IDRI (Lung Image Database Consortium and Image Database Resource Initiative)
privileged_info_summary: The human lungs consist of 5 anatomical lobes: 3 in the right lung and 2 in the left lung. The average total lung capacity for a healthy adult is approximately 6,000 ml, making 3,000 ml a valid minimum threshold for volumetric analysis. Anatomically, the right lung is larger than the left, with a volume ratio typically exceeding 1.0 (approximately 1.1 to 1.22). Standard physiological lobe proportions are approximately: Right Upper Lobe (RUL) 20%, Right Middle Lobe (RML) 8-9%, Right Lower Lobe (RLL) 25%, Left Upper Lobe (LUL) 22-25%, and Left Lower Lobe (LLL) 20-22%.
